title: primula lutea subsp. lutea
intro: Primula lutea subsp. lutea, also known as the yellow primrose or yellow cowslip, is a perennial plant species within the primrose family that is native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its chemical compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Primula lutea subsp. lutea can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies for its potential medicinal properties.
